parent.name: Brett Winton
parent.embed_html:
On the remarkable return on capital potential for Starlink on Starship.

Including customer acquisition cost, ground station capex, and an expendable top stage, we think SpaceX should be able to launch its 10th commercial starship rocket for ~$500m.

The bandwidth it launches… pic.twitter.com/VObM1ACgnT

— Brett Winton (@wintonARK) May 29, 2026

ai_summary: Ark Invest analyst discusses SpaceX's unit economics for Starlink deployment via Starship, estimating ~$500M launch costs by the 10th commercial flight when accounting for customer acquisition, ground infrastructure, and expendable stages, with Elon confirming significant profit potential. This reveals critical business strategy around SpaceX's path to profitability through reusable rocket economics and Starlink's satellite internet scalability, demonstrating how manufacturing cost reduction directly enables new revenue models.
embed_html:
Very big upside

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 30, 2026
